{"title":"Mitigated – Log Analytics and Application Insights intermittent data latency in West Europe","service":"Application Insights","region":"West Europe","communication":"

What happened?

Between 06:40 UTC and 12:45 UTC on 11 May 2026, a platform issue resulted in an impact to the Log Analytics and Application Insights service in the West Europe region. Impacted customers ingesting telemetry in their workspaces may have experienced intermittent data latency and incorrect alert activation for workspaces hosted in the region.


What do we know so far?

We determined that increased service demand caused the system to reach a scaling threshold, preventing it from automatically expanding capacity further. As a result, data processing could not keep pace with incoming volume, leading to a buildup in processing delays.


How did we respond?

  • 06:40 UTC on 11 May 2026 – Customer impact began.
  • 08:57 UTC on 11 May 2026 – We detected the issue via automated monitoring indicating elevated processing delays.
  • 09:15 UTC on 11 May 2026 – We determined that a surge in service demand pushed the system to its current capacity limit, preventing further automatic expansion and causing processing to fall behind.
  • 11:05 UTC on 11 May 2026 – Service capacity was increased beyond automatic limits to accelerate processing.
  • 12:45 UTC on 11 May 2026 – Data processing delays reduced, service performance returned to expected levels, and customer impact mitigated.


What happens next?

  • Our team will be completing an internal retrospective to understand the incident in more detail. Once that is completed, generally within 14 days, we will publish a Post Incident Review (PIR) to all impacted customers.
  • To get notified when that happens, and/or to stay informed about future Azure service issues, make sure that you configure and maintain Azure Service Health alerts – these can trigger emails, SMS, push notifications, webhooks, and more: https://aka.ms/ash-alerts
  • For more information on Post Incident Reviews, refer to https://aka.ms/AzurePIRs
  • The impact times above represent the full incident duration, so are not specific to any individual customer. Actual impact to service availability may vary between customers and resources – for guidance on implementing monitoring to understand granular impact: https://aka.ms/AzPIR/Monitoring
  • Finally, for broader guidance on preparing for cloud incidents, refer to https://aka.ms/incidentreadiness
